summary.intent: Merge main into the httpd-crate branch, resolve conflicts, fix review comments from greptile on PR #296 and PR #465, and clean up logs.txt from git history.
summary.outcome: All merges completed and conflicts resolved; greptile findings addressed across two PRs; logs.txt purged from history via git-filter-repo; a new PR #465 created after the original PR #296 was closed by the force-push rewrite.

repo: TLS setup (rustls crypto provider install_default) was moved from gateway to httpd crate as part of the httpd-crate refactor; it must be called in prepare_gateway to cover both start_gateway and prepare_gateway_embedded paths.
Browser warmup (start_browser_warmup_after_listener) was inadvertently dropped from the destructure pattern during the refactor; browser_tool_for_warmup must be threaded through BannerMeta.
Session event enrichment logic (active_channel preview, ChannelReplyTarget) was simplified/lost during the refactor and needed to be restored from main's gateway server.rs.
The proxy_shutdown_tx sender was being silently dropped by the .. destructure pattern, requiring it to be stored in PreparedGateway to keep the proxy alive.
The /ws WebSocket route alias was missing in httpd's router — the /websocket route existed but /ws was not registered.
release.yml had 4 redundant pre-version binary build steps across Linux pkg, AppImage, macOS, and Windows jobs — each job compiled twice before version was set.
moltis-agents was not a dependency of the httpd crate and needed to be added to Cargo.toml to reference AgentTool type when threading browser_tool_for_warmup.
git-filter-repo removes the origin remote as a safety measure after rewriting history.
Nightly rustfmt requires grouped use blocks (use { a::b, c::d }) rather than separate use statements.

code.finding: prepare_gateway is the correct place for rustls install_default() — covers both the normal start_gateway path and the embedded/swift-bridge path.
BannerMeta struct needed a browser_tool_for_warmup field to thread the tool from PreparedGatewayCore through to start_browser_warmup_after_listener.
A field in PreparedGateway (proxy_shutdown_tx) was private and needed to be made pub to allow httpd to store it.
Local MAX_LIFETIME constant was shadowed by module-level MAX_BROWSER_INSTANCE_LIFETIME; the local one was unused and could be removed.
Extra blank line before a test function caused nightly rustfmt check failure.
Imports from moltis_gateway and moltis_httpd needed to be grouped into a single use block to satisfy nightly rustfmt.

workflow: git-filter-repo is the recommended tool over filter-branch for removing files from history; it is faster and safer but removes origin remote as a side effect.
After git-filter-repo rewrites entire history, the branch loses shared ancestry with origin/main — force-pushing closes open PRs on GitHub and requires creating a new PR.
When filter-repo rewrites all commits (not just branch commits), the cleanest recovery is to diff the branch against main, create a new branch from origin/main, apply the diff, and squash-commit.
gh CLI requires a default repo set (gh repo set-default) when working in a worktree context without a standard origin.
Resolving greptile PR review threads requires the GitHub GraphQL API (resolveReviewThread mutation) since the REST API does not expose thread resolution.

friction: git-filter-repo silently removed the origin remote, causing the subsequent git push to fail and the PR to close unexpectedly.
The rewritten history had no common ancestor with origin/main, preventing a normal PR creation and requiring a squash-onto-main workaround.
gh CLI failed with 'No default remote repository' in the worktree context, requiring an explicit gh repo set-default call.
PR #296 was permanently closed by the force-push and could not be reopened (GitHub requires shared history), forcing creation of PR #465.
AgentTool type was not accessible in httpd crate — moltis-agents dependency was missing from Cargo.toml, causing a compile error mid-fix.
23 merge conflicts required parallel agent resolution across 3 groups.
A second merge shortly after introduced a test file (heartbeat_cron.rs) that needed both relocation and import fixes, plus a subsequent rustfmt fix in a separate commit.
open_items: PR #465 is a squash commit (single commit) rather than preserving the original branch commit history — future reviewers lose granular commit context.
The session event enrichment fix (P1 from PR #465) restored logic from main but should be verified end-to-end for correctness of ChannelReplyTarget and active_channel preview behavior.
The release.yml redundant build removal (P2) was applied but CI has not been confirmed green — worth monitoring the next release run.
logs.txt was removed from history but the PR description or team should be notified that the branch SHA changed and any local clones need to be re-fetched.
